bug_summary.title: Full Bottle Variant Not Displaying Correctly on Product Page
bug_summary.affected_product: Creed Aventus (and any product with multiple variant types)
bug_summary.description: When a product has both a 5ml subscription variant ($20/month) and a full-size bottle variant (3.3 fl oz, $408), the product page always displays the subscription view regardless of which variant is selected in the dropdown.
bug_summary.expected_behavior: Selecting the full bottle variant should show the retail price ($408), compare-at price ($510), savings badge, and an 'Add to Cart' button instead of the subscription UI.
bug_summary.actual_behavior: The page always shows $20/month pricing, 5ml atomizer visual, subscription benefits, and 'Add to My Queue' button — even when '3.3 fl oz' is selected.
bug_summary.revenue_impact: Blocks all full-bottle sales ($408+ per unit). Customers cannot see the correct price or purchase flow for full-size products.

issues.description: The entire product info section is wrapped in a 'subscription-box' div that hardcodes the subscription UI (5ml atomizer SVG, $20/month price from settings, subscription benefits list, 'Add to My Queue' button). There is NO conditional logic to detect variant type.

No variant change event listener exists. The JavaScript handles gallery thumbnails and form submission but never listens for changes on the .variant-select dropdown. The hidden #variantId input is never updated when the user changes the variant.
Product variant data (prices, availability, compare-at prices, options) is not serialized into JavaScript. The frontend has no access to variant details needed for dynamic UI updates.

screenshots.description: Frontend showing Creed Aventus product page. SIZE dropdown shows '3.3 fl oz' selected, but page still displays $20/month subscription view with 'ADD TO MY QUEUE' button.
Shopify admin showing the 3.3 fl oz variant correctly configured: Price $408.00, Compare at $510.00, Cost $240.00. Two variants visible: 5ml and 3.3 fl oz.

evidence.conclusion: Data is correct in Shopify admin. The bug is 100% in the frontend template rendering.

fix_instructions.overview: Single-file fix in sections/main-product.liquid with 4 changes: Liquid variant detection logic, conditional HTML rendering, new CSS styles, and JavaScript variant change handler.

changes.title: Add Variant Detection Logic
changes.location: Inside the opening {%- liquid ... -%} block (lines 1-58), add before the closing -%}
changes.description: Add Liquid logic to detect whether the current variant is a subscription (5ml) or one-time purchase (full bottle). Also detect if the product has both variant types.
changes.logic: Loop through product.variants, check each variant.title for subscription keywords. If title contains any keyword, it's a subscription variant. If not, it's a full-bottle variant.

changes.title: Add Conditional Rendering for Purchase Views
changes.description: Split into three sections: (1) Subscription View div with id='subscriptionView' that shows/hides based on variant type, (2) Purchase View div with id='purchaseView' for full-bottle variants, (3) Shared form section used by both views.

contains: Purchase details (full-size bottle, one-time purchase, free shipping)
Dynamic submit button ('Add to My Queue' or 'Add to Cart')

changes.title: Add CSS Styles for Purchase Box
changes.description: Add styles for the new .purchase-box, .purchase-box__header, .purchase-box__pricing, .compare-price, .savings-badge, .purchase-box__details, .purchase-detail, and .product-purchase-form classes. Include mobile responsive styles.

changes.title: Replace JavaScript with Variant-Aware Handler
changes.description: Complete rewrite of the JavaScript section to add variant change handling.

new_features.name: Variant Data Serialization
new_features.description: Serialize all product.variants into a JavaScript array with id, title, price, priceFormatted, compareAtPrice, compareAtPriceFormatted, available, options, and featuredImage.

new_features.name: isSubscriptionVariant() Helper
new_features.description: Function that checks if a variant title contains subscription keywords (5ml, atomizer, etc.).

new_features.name: onVariantChange() Handler
new_features.description: Main handler that: (1) reads selected options from all dropdowns, (2) finds matching variant from serialized data, (3) updates hidden variant ID input, (4) toggles subscription vs purchase view visibility, (5) updates button text, (6) updates price/compare-at/savings for purchase view, (7) updates product image if variant has specific image, (8) updates URL with variant parameter.

new_features.name: Event Binding
new_features.description: Binds 'change' event to all .variant-select dropdowns to trigger onVariantChange().

new_features.name: Preserved Features
new_features.description: Gallery thumbnail clicks, add-to-cart form submission, cart drawer opening, and copy link button all remain functional.

variant_naming_convention.description: The JavaScript detection relies on variant title matching. This convention must be followed for all future variants.

tests: Page loads with subscription view ($20/month, 5ml visual, benefits list)
'Add to My Queue' button displayed
No variant dropdown shown (single variant)
Add to cart works correctly

testing_checklist.scenario: Product with BOTH 5ml and full bottle variants (Creed Aventus)
tests: Page loads with correct view for default variant
Variant dropdown displays both options
Selecting '5ml' shows subscription view with $20/month
Selecting '3.3 fl oz' shows purchase view with $408.00
Compare-at price ($510.00) shown with strikethrough
Savings badge shows 'Save $102.00'
Button changes between 'Add to My Queue' and 'Add to Cart'
Switching variants back and forth works smoothly
Add to cart works for both variant types
Loading with ?variant=[full-bottle-id] shows purchase view
Loading with ?variant=[5ml-id] shows subscription view
Out-of-stock full bottle shows 'Sold Out' disabled button
Out-of-stock 5ml shows 'Sold Out' disabled button
Purchase box looks correct on mobile (375px)
Variant dropdown usable on touch devices
Price and savings badge properly visible

steps: Go to Products > Creed Aventus > Variants
Confirm 5ml variant exists with correct price ($20.00)
Confirm 3.3 fl oz variant exists with Price: $408.00, Compare at: $510.00
Verify both variants have 'Track quantity' enabled
Verify both variants have stock levels set

backward_compatibility.existing_products: All products with only 5ml variants will continue to work exactly as before. No changes needed.
backward_compatibility.new_behavior: Only activates when a product has multiple variant types (subscription + full bottle).
backward_compatibility.future_products: Any new product with both variant types will automatically get the dual-view functionality, as long as the naming convention is followed.
